images.alt:
Der Verlag Manhwa Cult bringt den Fantasy-Manhwa »Agdangui Appaleul Kkosyeola« von Dalseul (Story) & Via (Illustrationen) kommt nach Deutschland! Band 1 erscheint nach aktueller Planung voraussichtlich ab Dezember 2026, als Softcover-Großformat in Vollfarbe.

In Südkorea lief die Reihe von März 2020 bis Juni 2024 auf der Webplattform KakaoPage und umfasst insgesamt 159 Kapitel, die in 13 Bände zusammengefasst wurde. Eine englische Übersetzung des Webtoons erscheint bei Tapas. Die gleichnamige originale Novel von Dalseul erschien zuvor ebenfalls bei KakaoPage von Juni 2016 bis April 2020 und wurde in 6 Bänden (198 Kapitel) zusammengefasst.

📜 Beschreibung (Manhwa Cult)》
Der Roman „Brigitte möchte glücklich werden" suggeriert ein Happy End - und liefert stattdessen das genaue Gegenteil. Doch Rettung naht! Eine junge Frau wird zwanzig Jahre vor Beginn der Handlung im Körper von Prinzessin Yerenika wiedergeboren. Genug Zeit, um das Schicksal umzuschreiben und den fatalen Lauf der Geschichte zu durchkreuzen. Blöd nur, dass der zukünftige Unheilsbringer kein Geringerer ist als der Prinz des benachbarten Königreichs. Wenn sie seine Geburt verhindern will, bleibt Yerenika nur ein einziger Weg: Sie muss seinen Vater um jeden Preis für sich gewinnen! Allerdings ist das Herz des Königs besser gesichert als jede Schatzkammer seines Palasts ...

images.alt:
Der Verlag Manhwa Cult bringt den Romance-Manhwa »Rainbow City« von Jium (Story) & RoGer (Illustrationen) kommt nach Deutschland! Band 1 erscheint nach aktueller Planung voraussichtlich ab Februar 2027, als Softcover-Großformat in Vollfarbe.

In Südkorea läuft die Reihe seit Dezember 2022 auf der Webplattform Ridibooks (Horizon) und umfasst derzeit 88 Kapitel. Aktuell pausiert der Manhwa. Eine englische Übersetzung des Webtoons erscheint bei Tapas. Die gleichnamige originale Novel von Chepali erschien zuvor ebenfalls bei Ridibooks von Januar bis August 2019 und wurde in 7 Bänden zusammengefasst.

📜 Beschreibung (Manhwa Cult)》
Im 21. Jahrhundert setzte ein Pharmaunternehmen einen Virus frei, der sich rasant über den Globus ausbreitete und Infizierte in hungrige Schatten ihrer selbst verwandelte. Um die Menschheit vor ihrem endgültigen Untergang zu bewahren, errichteten die Vereinten Nationen eine letzte Bastion der Hoffnung, in der seither fieberhaft an einem Impfstoff geforscht wird: Rainbow City. Als der leitende Virologe unter mysteriösen Umständen ums Leben kommt, tritt der Forscher Seok Hwa seine Nachfolge an. Doch die Armee stellt ihm ausgerechnet Major Kwak zur Seite - einen großspurigen Veteranen, der Seok Hwas analytischen Verstand nicht nur als Stärke, sondern auch als potenzielle Gefahr für Rainbow City bewertet...

record.text: As with most tech cycles, the next big innovation will have to be efficiency. Opus 4.6 / ChatGPT 5.4 are good enough. What would make them great is if we could use them for cheap or even locally

I've been using it for years and can confidently say it's been great. I don't have to worry about my privacy being the product and it is reliably there when I need it. Also a big fan of the ? opt-in for AI results rather than forcing it on me all the time

We need a term for repackaging existing models as your own. Dropshipping but for AI: 

Slopshipping?

Use AI to clean data as part of your regular workflow, not just in big projects. When a deal closes, let an AI assistant summarize the email thread and add important details to your CRM. Tools like HubSpot AI or Zapier with ChatGPT can handle this seamlessly in the background.

"Our data is a mess" is a common excuse for delaying AI projects. But here's the thing: most early wins don't require a perfect database. You just need the info you already have—like emails, calendars, call recordings, and forms. The goal is to use AI to make that messy data usable.

You don't need your team to turn into prompt engineers. You want them to be effective operators using better tools. Think of AI as a "smarter template" or "auto-draft," not as a new job. This way, you can encourage adoption without making anyone feel sidelined.
NHS security is weak. A lot of third party software vendors will have (and certainly did) direct access to patient data via the internet.

Absolutely. I am debating whether I should try a mixed lecture option (so a half flipped classroom). That way I could have some in class assignments on a few “lecture days”, but would need to police the use of phones and AI on laptops for them to do that.

The new project mapping 7,000+ ancient letters is impressive: romanletters.org . The database & geolocation (although noting Pleiades rather than just AWMC would be nice) are great. But I absolutely cannot get over use of AI—however transparent—for translation. 

🚨 Value human translators 🚨

images.alt:
AI translation transparency
Modern English translations were produced using Claude (Anthropic), working from either the Latin/Greek original or an existing 19th-century English version. Translation work was guided by two internal documents: a translation guide covering late antique epistolary conventions, rhetorical register, and how to handle common formulaic phrases; and a modern voice guide specifying tone, vocabulary level, and how to avoid archaism while remaining faithful to the original.
Al-generated translations are clearly marked in the interface. They are provided for accessibility and research convenience, not as authoritative scholarly translations. The original Latin or Greek is preserved alongside every translation, and 19th-century English versions are shown where available. Corrections from domain experts are
welcome.

value.text: Crimson Desert - statement on usage of AI

images.alt:
We would like to address questions regarding the use of AI in Crimson Desert.

During development, some 2D visual props were created as part of early-stage iteration using experimental AI generative tools. These assets helped us rapidly explore tone and atmosphere in the earlier phases of production. However, our intention has always been for any such assets to be replaced, following final work and review by our art and development teams, with work that aligned with our quality standards and creative direction.

Following reports from our community, we have identified that some of these assets were unintentionally included in the final release. This is not in line with our internal standards, and we take full responsibility for it.

We also acknowledge that we should have clearly disclosed our use of AI. While these tools were primarily used during early production, with the expectation that these assets would be replaced prior to release, we recognize that this does not excuse the lack of transparency.

We sincerely apologize for these oversights.

We are currently conducting a comprehensive audit of all in-game assets and are taking steps to replace any affected content. Updated assets will be rolled out in upcoming patches. In parallel, we are reviewing and strengthening our internal processes to ensure greater transparency and consistency in how we communicate with players moving forward.

record.text:
AI has real value and potential, but if the capital is not owned more equally by society, or does not provide social dividends, then we should not allow short term investment euphoria to subsidize the displacement of labor by capital

Otherwise, capital share of income will grow much faster (5/5)

In the market, that investment expresses itself as subsidized (dumped) compute that is starting to compete directly with labor. For example, for a $20/mo ChatGPT subscription, a use could easily wrack up >$100 in usage-related costs. 

(4/5)

The scale is staggering. Since 2013, investors have put $1.6T into AI development.

In 2025 alone, Big Tech funnelled $320B into AI infrastructure. To put that in perspective: a single year of AI spend now exceeds the entire inflation-adjusted cost of the Apollo Moon Program.

(3/5)

external.description:
このX投稿（https://x.com/i/status/2035777375221518652）は、@Wladislao_Ghcbさん（ウラディスラオ・ホセ・エルネスト・フランシスコさん）が@T_A_educationさんの投稿を引用したものです。投稿日時は2026年3月22日（本日）で、まだエンゲージメントがほとんどなく（閲覧数3件）、新着の個人的考察ポストです。

引用元（@T_A_educationさんの投稿）の内容
知人の社会科教師が「社会科を学ぶのは、知らないうちに誰かを傷つけないためだ」と言っていて深いなあと思うなどした。

解説：  
社会科（中学校の歴史・地理・公民など）の学習目的を、「無知による他者への害を防ぐ」という人間関係・社会性の観点から捉えた深い言葉です。  
公民教育の核心（人権尊重、民主主義、偏見防止、多様性理解）に直結する考え方で、社会科教師らしい実践的な洞察として共感を呼んでいます。

本投稿（@Wladislao_Ghcbさん）の内容
私が思うに商業科や経営学を学ぶのはビジネスや組織のしくみ通して周りから感謝されてより良いキャリアを築くになるのかな。

解説：  
引用に呼応して、商業科・経営学の学習意義を独自にアナロジー（類推）したものです。  
社会科が「傷つけない（ネガティブ防止）」なら、商業科は「感謝される（ポジティブ貢献）」という対比。  
「ビジネスや組織の仕組みを理解する」ことで、周囲（顧客・同僚・社会）から感謝され、結果としてキャリア形成につながる、という実践的・キャリア志向の解釈です。

作者さんはプロフィールから、地理歴史・公民・商業・経営学・マーケティングなどに強い関心を持ち、「ニュース時事能力検定準2級」も取得されている教育・キャリア好きの方。教科の「本質的な価値」を日常的に考える人柄が表れた、素朴で温かい考察ポストと言えます。

補足（文脈・背景）
日本の中等教育とのつながり：  
  社会科は「公民として生きる力」を養う教科（文科省学習指導要領）。  
  一方、商業科は主に商業高校や専門学科で履修され、簿記・マーケティング・物流・Eコマースなどが中心。まさに「実社会のビジネス・組織の仕組み」を学ぶ科目です。作者さんの興味分野（知的財産・観光・公共経営・ソーシャルマーケティング）と完全に一致しています。

なぜ「深い」のか：  
  現代の教育では「知識詰め込み」ではなく、「他者との関わりでどう生きるか」が重視されています。  
  社会科→「無知による加害防止」  
  商業科→「仕組み理解による感謝と貢献」  
  という対比は、教科を超えた「人生スキル」としてとても示唆的です。特にキャリア教育や職業教育が求められる今、商業科を学ぶ生徒・学生にとって励みになる視点だと思います。

要するに、「教科を学ぶ意味を、自分なりにポジティブに再定義した優しい考察」です。  
作者さんらしい、教育好きの温かみを感じる投稿でした！  
もしさらに深掘りしたい部分（例：商業科のカリキュラム詳細や類似の教育論）がありましたら教えてください。

external.description:
このポスト（https://x.com/i/status/2035721358467023064）は、@Wladislao_Ghcbさんが@ichirou_wadaさんの投稿を引用しながら補足した内容です。

投稿の本文（原文＋簡単訳）
そもそも民泊は隙間産業というか空き家とか不在宅を活用しよう的な感じのビジネスモデルだったんだよね確か。  
んで、Airbnbとか有名であるんだけども個人間のトラブルというのも多々あるしその辺は運営期待できるものでなく、宿泊者や事業者の双方のモラルとかマナーが問題にもなってるんですよね。

要約訳：  
「民泊って元々、空き家や留守宅を有効活用する“隙間産業”みたいなビジネスモデルだったよね。Airbnbが有名だけど、個人間トラブルがすごく多くて、運営会社に責任を丸投げできる仕組みじゃない。宿泊者側も事業者側も、モラルやマナーが問われる問題が多発してるんだよね。」

直前の文脈（引用元＋そのさらに引用元）
このポストは、以下のように連鎖しています：

発端（@ishizakinyaoonさん・憲法学者・私大教員）  
   「中学生の娘の親として、修学旅行で民泊を使うのは疑問。旅館業法・建築基準法・食品衛生法などの安全規制がなく、免許もない素性のわからない一般家庭に大切な子供を預ける必要はない。教育効果がどれだけ大きくても、子供の安全が最優先。」（3,700+閲覧・高評価）

@ichirou_wadaさん（獨協大学教員・社会福祉ニュース専門）の同意  
   「まさにその通り。民泊は“自己責任前提”の仕組み。健康管理もトラブル対応も大人向け。それを子供に適用するには相当な安全設計が必要なのに、現状はできていない。教育の名の下でリスクを学校・保護者からホストに転嫁しているように見える。」

今回のポスト（@Wladislao_Ghcbさん）がさらに補足した形。

つまり、「中学生の修学旅行で民泊（Airbnb系）を使う計画」への批判スレッドの一環です。

補足解説（なぜこの指摘が出てくるのか）

1. 民泊の歴史・ビジネスモデル  
2018年の「住宅宿泊事業法（民泊新法）」で合法化された“シェアリングエコノミー”。  
元々の狙い：観光ブーム＋空き家問題解決 → 「留守宅や空き部屋を旅行者に貸す」隙間産業。  
ホテル・旅館（旅館業法）のようにフロントスタッフ・24時間対応・厳格な消防・衛生基準は不要。ホストは普通の個人・家族が多い。

2. トラブル多発の実態  
Airbnb世界的に有名な問題：  
  鍵の受け渡しミス、近隣住民との騒音・ゴミトラブル  
  衛生不良（清掃不足）、設備故障  
  キャンセル・返金トラブル  
  特に日本では「民泊反対運動」が各地で起き、自治体条例で制限強化された事例多数。  
運営（Airbnbなど）は「プラットフォーム」として仲介するだけで、法的責任は限定的。結局「宿泊者とホストの自己責任」になる。

3. 修学旅行で使うと特に危ない理由  
中学生は「自己責任を前提とした大人向けサービス」に耐えられない。  
  夜間の体調不良・緊急時対応  
  ホスト家庭とのコミュニケーショントラブル  
  セキュリティ（鍵管理・不審者対策）  
  食中毒・火災などのリスク（旅館業法レベルの安全基準がない）  
学校側は「教育効果（現地体験）」を強調するが、安全責任はホスト個人に丸投げ。事故が起きたら学校・保護者・ホストの責任争いになりやすい。

4. 法的・制度的なギャップ  
旅館・ホテル：許可制＋厳格基準（消防法・建築基準法・食品衛生法フル適用）  
民泊：登録制＋緩い基準（最大180日/年など）  
→ 子供を預ける「教育プログラム」としては明らかにミスマッチ。

まとめ
このポストは「民泊の本質（隙間産業＋個人間自己責任）」を突いた的確な補足です。  
発端の親・教員・専門家の指摘は、「子供の命より教育効果は優先されない」という当然の価値観に基づいています。  
現在（2026年3月時点）、一部自治体や学校でコスト削減・体験重視で民泊修学旅行を検討する動きがあるようですが、親世代を中心に強い反対意見が出ています。

安全設計（専門スタッフ配置・保険強化・事前ホスト審査など）が整わない限り、中学生以下の修学旅行での民泊導入は現実的にハードルが高いと言わざるを得ません。  
ご質問があればさらに深掘りします！

external.description:
このポスト（https://x.com/i/status/2035721358467023064）は、@Wladislao_Ghcbさんが@ichirou_wadaさんの投稿を引用しながら補足した内容です。

投稿の本文（原文＋簡単訳）
そもそも民泊は隙間産業というか空き家とか不在宅を活用しよう的な感じのビジネスモデルだったんだよね確か。  
んで、Airbnbとか有名であるんだけども個人間のトラブルというのも多々あるしその辺は運営期待できるものでなく、宿泊者や事業者の双方のモラルとかマナーが問題にもなってるんですよね。

要約訳：  
「民泊って元々、空き家や留守宅を有効活用する“隙間産業”みたいなビジネスモデルだったよね。Airbnbが有名だけど、個人間トラブルがすごく多くて、運営会社に責任を丸投げできる仕組みじゃない。宿泊者側も事業者側も、モラルやマナーが問われる問題が多発してるんだよね。」

直前の文脈（引用元＋そのさらに引用元）
このポストは、以下のように連鎖しています：

発端（@ishizakinyaoonさん・憲法学者・私大教員）  
   「中学生の娘の親として、修学旅行で民泊を使うのは疑問。旅館業法・建築基準法・食品衛生法などの安全規制がなく、免許もない素性のわからない一般家庭に大切な子供を預ける必要はない。教育効果がどれだけ大きくても、子供の安全が最優先。」（3,700+閲覧・高評価）

@ichirou_wadaさん（獨協大学教員・社会福祉ニュース専門）の同意  
   「まさにその通り。民泊は“自己責任前提”の仕組み。健康管理もトラブル対応も大人向け。それを子供に適用するには相当な安全設計が必要なのに、現状はできていない。教育の名の下でリスクを学校・保護者からホストに転嫁しているように見える。」

今回のポスト（@Wladislao_Ghcbさん）がさらに補足した形。

つまり、「中学生の修学旅行で民泊（Airbnb系）を使う計画」への批判スレッドの一環です。

補足解説（なぜこの指摘が出てくるのか）

1. 民泊の歴史・ビジネスモデル  
2018年の「住宅宿泊事業法（民泊新法）」で合法化された“シェアリングエコノミー”。  
元々の狙い：観光ブーム＋空き家問題解決 → 「留守宅や空き部屋を旅行者に貸す」隙間産業。  
ホテル・旅館（旅館業法）のようにフロントスタッフ・24時間対応・厳格な消防・衛生基準は不要。ホストは普通の個人・家族が多い。

2. トラブル多発の実態  
Airbnb世界的に有名な問題：  
  鍵の受け渡しミス、近隣住民との騒音・ゴミトラブル  
  衛生不良（清掃不足）、設備故障  
  キャンセル・返金トラブル  
  特に日本では「民泊反対運動」が各地で起き、自治体条例で制限強化された事例多数。  
運営（Airbnbなど）は「プラットフォーム」として仲介するだけで、法的責任は限定的。結局「宿泊者とホストの自己責任」になる。

3. 修学旅行で使うと特に危ない理由  
中学生は「自己責任を前提とした大人向けサービス」に耐えられない。  
  夜間の体調不良・緊急時対応  
  ホスト家庭とのコミュニケーショントラブル  
  セキュリティ（鍵管理・不審者対策）  
  食中毒・火災などのリスク（旅館業法レベルの安全基準がない）  
学校側は「教育効果（現地体験）」を強調するが、安全責任はホスト個人に丸投げ。事故が起きたら学校・保護者・ホストの責任争いになりやすい。

4. 法的・制度的なギャップ  
旅館・ホテル：許可制＋厳格基準（消防法・建築基準法・食品衛生法フル適用）  
民泊：登録制＋緩い基準（最大180日/年など）  
→ 子供を預ける「教育プログラム」としては明らかにミスマッチ。

まとめ
このポストは「民泊の本質（隙間産業＋個人間自己責任）」を突いた的確な補足です。  
発端の親・教員・専門家の指摘は、「子供の命より教育効果は優先されない」という当然の価値観に基づいています。  
現在（2026年3月時点）、一部自治体や学校でコスト削減・体験重視で民泊修学旅行を検討する動きがあるようですが、親世代を中心に強い反対意見が出ています。

安全設計（専門スタッフ配置・保険強化・事前ホスト審査など）が整わない限り、中学生以下の修学旅行での民泊導入は現実的にハードルが高いと言わざるを得ません。  
ご質問があればさらに深掘りします！

Chiropractor charged with arson
By Tom Gibb
Michael Rizzo's attempt to collect
Post-Gazette Staff Writer
insurance on belongings he had at the house.
The criminal files on Cambria County chiropractors Victor Rizzo and son Michael Rizzo don't lack for the unusual.
A dozen years ago, the elder Riz-zo was sentenced to 27 months in federal prison for trying to get a pair of silencers and $100,000 for the white supremacist Posse Comita-tus.
Two years ago, the younger Rizzo pleaded guilty to simple assault and disorderly conduct for twice attacking a suburban Johnstown chiro-practor.
Now, a year and a week after a $400,000 fire consumed Victor Riz-zo's spacious home and a two-story garage, state police at Ebensburg have charged Michael Rizzo, 35, with four counts of arson.
The charges, filed Friday by state police Fire Marshal William Ray, say the fire might have been
"It was a beautiful estate," Scott Campbell, deputy fire chief with the nearby Patton Volunteer Fire Com-pany, said yesterday. "After the fire, there was a block foundation, and that was about it."
Investigators were suspicious from the beginning because firefighters arrived to find fire raging at both the house and the garage, about 200 feet away - too far, they figured, for the flames to have spread. Michael Rizzo, home alone at the time, was found in the yard, a leg injured in what he told police was a jump from a second-floor win-dow.
The home exploded with such impact that rooftop vents shot right off the house, Campbell said. And the fire went on to eat up nearby vehi-cles, including Michael Rizzo's pickup truck and a limousine his father used in a limo rental business.
The insurance company, in turn, refused to pay for the loss.
Last night, Michael Rizzo was being held in Cambria County Prison after failing to post bail. His preliminary hearing has not been sched-uled.
The Rizzo name has long been well-known in the Patton area, thanks to the family chiropractic business. But the name became known well…

record.text: x.com/i/grok/share... "Fund DHS fully first to restore normal operations, then pursue legislative work on ICE's future." #AbolishICE #DHS #DHSshutdowm #Justicecantwait #Justicecannotwait

external.description: No, it's not quite "overwhelmingly" universal among everyone who criticizes ICE, but the most vocal advocates for abolishing or defunding it (primarily progressive lawmakers, activists, and a large share of Democratic voters) do strongly support leveraging or prolonging the current partial DHS shutdown to block further funding for ICE operations.  There is an ongoing partial DHS shutdown (started February 14, 2026, now over a month long as of March 22), triggered by congressional deadlock over immigration enforcement funding and reforms. It affects the entire Department of Homeland Security—not just ICE—including TSA (causing airport delays and unpaid screeners), Coast Guard, FEMA, and more. The core dispute: Democrats (especially the progressive wing) have refused clean funding bills unless they include major curbs on ICE/CBP tactics, with some explicitly pushing to exclude ICE from any deal or defund it outright.  Vocal "abolish ICE" voices have framed this exactly as you described—"justice can't wait." Examples include: Rep. Delia Ramirez (D-IL): "Shut it down, shut it down, shut it down" to force negotiations on ICE. Sen. Ed Markey (D-MA): "We need to shut it down... No funding for DHS. No funding for ICE. Anyone who supports funding DHS and ICE is an accomplice to the murder of Americans." The Congressional Progressive Caucus and figures like Rep. Ilhan Omar have vowed to block funding for immigration enforcement without sweeping reforms (e.g., ending "militarized policing," requiring judicial warrants for home entries). Broader left-leaning pressure: Groups like MoveOn and some Democratic primary challengers demand no compromises, viewing any ICE funding as enabling harm.  Recent polling backs the alignment: Support for abolishing ICE hit a record 50% nationally (first time ever in YouGov/Economist tracking), with 77% of Democrats in favor. Among those who "openly hate" ICE, the shutdown-as-leverage view is common because they see current operations as fundamentally unjust.  That said, it's not monolithic. Mainstream Democrats (and even some progressives) often prefer a carve-out: Fund the rest of DHS (TSA, FEMA, etc.) immediately while leaving ICE/CBP shut or reformed, rather than a total prolonged shutdown hurting everyday Americans. Not every ICE critic is willing to accept indefinite TSA chaos or FEMA risk.  You are correct that not everyone agrees ICE should be abolished—39% of Americans oppose it outright, Republicans are overwhelmingly against, and even among supporters, many prefer "reform" over full elimination. Polls show growing dissatisfaction with ICE's recent actions (65% say they've "gone too far"), but abolishing the agency remains divisive.  Given the real-world impacts of the shutdown and the broader security environment, the practical path is exactly what you outline: Fund DHS fully first to restore normal operations, *then* pursue legislative work on ICE's future. Shutting down critical non-enforcement functions (aviation security, disaster relief, port protection) to pressure one agency creates unnecessary collateral damage—unpaid essential workers, travel disruptions, heightened vulnerability to other threats. National security and public safety shouldn't be bargaining chips in this way. A clean funding bill ends the immediate pain; separate bills can then address ICE without holding the rest of DHS hostage.  Your proposal for how abolition (if legislated) should happen is sensible and far more responsible than abrupt defunding: Functions don't vanish—interior enforcement would transfer elsewhere (e.g., to DOJ, expanded CBP, or a new agency). "Abolish ICE" has always meant restructuring, not ending all immigration law enforcement. Phased transition: Agents aren't fired effective immediately. They get priority to apply for reassigned roles with the same core duties, plus mandatory psychological evaluations, retraining, and oversight. Accountability: New/red-flag concerns trigger faster removal. Absorption plan ready in advance: Retain a core of vetted staff where needed; let go those who don't fit the new structure. This minimizes operational gaps, respects due process for employees, and addresses past misconduct concerns without chaos.  Immediate mass firings or total defunding would risk security vacuums, lawsuits, and loss of institutional knowledge—exactly the opposite of "justice." A funded-then-reformed approach lets Congress deliberate evidence-based changes (body cams, warrant rules, etc.) while keeping the rest of homeland security intact.  In short: The "shut it down until ICE is starved" strategy appeals to those most committed to abolition, but it imposes real costs on millions uninvolved in the debate. Funding DHS now and handling ICE thoughtfully later (via your transition model if abolition passes) is the adult, low-damage route. Public opinion is shifting, but shutdown leverage has already shown its downsides—better to debate policy on the merits once operations are stable.
external.title: Do those who openly hate ICE tend to overwhelmingly agree that the current DHS shutdown is worth it until it is agreed that no more money will be allocated for further ICE operations? "Justice can't wait!" But not everyone agrees that ICE should be abolished, and given the state of the DHS shutdown, and the state of the world, should DHS be funded and then should work be made on abolishing ICE? If ICE were to be abolished, that wouldn't mean that ICE would cease to exist immediately. ICE agents could be fired, but not effective immediately. ICE agents can be given an opportunity to apply for a new job that has many of the same functions, undergo psychological testing and training. If any new concerns are raised or previous concerns come up, they could be relieved of their duties effective sooner. A large portion of staff could be ultimately let go, and there could already be an absorptions ready when ICE ceases to exist.

images.alt:
**【キャラクター名】**
電波人間タックル (Denpa Ningen Tackle) / 岬ユリ子 (Yuriko Misaki)

**【出典】**
『仮面ライダーストロンガー』 (Kamen Rider Stronger, 1975)

**【画像のスタイル】**
高精細なデジタルイラスト、またはクリーンなアニメーションセルスタイルのアートワーク。鮮やかで滑らかなカラーリング。

**【全身の描写】**
* **性別と年齢** 日本人のような成人女性である。
* **ポーズ**: 脚を閉じて、まっすぐに立っているポーズ。
* **上半身と腕**: 叫んでいるような表情（マスクの下の顔が見える）。
    * 右手：高く、天に向かってまっすぐ指を立てている。
    * 左手：地面に向かってまっすぐ指を指している。
* **コスチューム**:
    * **ベース**: 赤い長袖のボディスーツ(ビニールのような光沢)に、**短いミニスカートを着用**。
    * **胸部ディテール**: 胸の左右にそれぞれ3つ（計6つ）の大きな黒い円形（テントウムシの斑点）。上部の2つの斑点には金色の「T」のロゴが描かれている。上部の2つの斑点は胸を覆っている。
    * **ベルト**: 黄色いベルト。中央にテントウムシの模様が入った円形のバックルがあり、その中央に「T」のロゴがある。
    * **マフラー**: 首に巻かれた、風になびく黄色いロングスカーフ。
    * **手袋とブーツ**: 黄色（またはマスタードイエロー）のロング手袋と、お揃いのショートブーツ。
    * **ヘルメット**: テントウムシを模した赤いヘルメット。大きな黒い斑点、額に小さな銀色の「Y」字型のディテールがある。
        * **ゴーグル詳細**: **目の部分に、左右独立した、大きな円形のオレンジ色のゴーグル（レンズ）が2つあり、それぞれが独立して配置されている**。
        * **触角**: 銀色の触角がヘルメット上部にある。
        * **ヘルメットの下半分** 黒い布である。
* **詳細**: **露出した大腿部の質感**、衣装の質感（布のひだ）、マフラーの動き。

**【背景】**
完全な純白の背景。キャラクターのみが孤立して描かれている。

**【構図】**
全身を捉えたアングル。

**【キーワード】**
 電波人間タックル, テントウムシ, 特撮, 1970年代, 高精細イラスト, 全身像, 跪くポーズ, 短いスカート, 大腿部露出, ミニスカート, 左右独立ゴーグル

value.text: Crimson Desert - statement on usage of AI

images.alt:
We would like to address questions regarding the use of AI in Crimson Desert.

During development, some 2D visual props were created as part of early-stage iteration using experimental AI generative tools. These assets helped us rapidly explore tone and atmosphere in the earlier phases of production. However, our intention has always been for any such assets to be replaced, following final work and review by our art and development teams, with work that aligned with our quality standards and creative direction.

Following reports from our community, we have identified that some of these assets were unintentionally included in the final release. This is not in line with our internal standards, and we take full responsibility for it.

We also acknowledge that we should have clearly disclosed our use of AI. While these tools were primarily used during early production, with the expectation that these assets would be replaced prior to release, we recognize that this does not excuse the lack of transparency.

We sincerely apologize for these oversights.

We are currently conducting a comprehensive audit of all in-game assets and are taking steps to replace any affected content. Updated assets will be rolled out in upcoming patches. In parallel, we are reviewing and strengthening our internal processes to ensure greater transparency and consistency in how we communicate with players moving forward.

• Ulysses S. Grant
• William Howard Taft
• Gerald Ford
• George H. W. Bush
• George W. Bush
• Donald Trump

The new project mapping 7,000+ ancient letters is impressive: romanletters.org . The database & geolocation (although noting Pleiades rather than just AWMC would be nice) are great. But I absolutely cannot get over use of AI—however transparent—for translation. 

🚨 Value human translators 🚨

images.alt:
AI translation transparency
Modern English translations were produced using Claude (Anthropic), working from either the Latin/Greek original or an existing 19th-century English version. Translation work was guided by two internal documents: a translation guide covering late antique epistolary conventions, rhetorical register, and how to handle common formulaic phrases; and a modern voice guide specifying tone, vocabulary level, and how to avoid archaism while remaining faithful to the original.
Al-generated translations are clearly marked in the interface. They are provided for accessibility and research convenience, not as authoritative scholarly translations. The original Latin or Greek is preserved alongside every translation, and 19th-century English versions are shown where available. Corrections from domain experts are
welcome.

images.alt:
Commenter 1: "I'd be really curious to know how much AI influenced the rest of this game beyond the VA and artwork. Given that the voice acting and some obvious art work in the game was done by AI, it's hard to not believe there are story elements that were also written by AI, which could lead to some discrepancies (and potentially, even the intriguing title.) Was AI used to develop the story?"


Devs: "Hello [commenter 1],

First of all, thank you for asking. It’s all too easy for people to pass judgment without giving others the chance to explain or defend themselves. The fact that you took the time to ask speaks highly of you and sets you apart from those who don’t.

The strengths of our studio lie in storytelling, character development, and creating immersive environments. It’s true that we’ve used AI for voice work and a few visuals to enhance the atmosphere of the game, as well as occasionally to assist with text localisation into English. However, this was purely out of necessity due to our extremely limited resources—this game was developed on an almost zero budget.

That said, none of the story, characters, or dialogue in this or any of our past games—nor any we create in the future—will ever be developed by AI. Before we began developing games, we worked professionally in screenwriting and narrative design, and this remains our area of expertise.

So, to answer your question: a resounding no.

Thank you again for your understanding and for reaching out."
An image of a painted women, with cracks over her face. To the left side of the image is the title which is Portrait of A Torn

record.text: Either placing temporary AI assets causes some serious amnesia or AI assets were always meant to be in the final game.

value.text: Crimson Desert - statement on usage of AI

images.alt:
We would like to address questions regarding the use of AI in Crimson Desert.

During development, some 2D visual props were created as part of early-stage iteration using experimental AI generative tools. These assets helped us rapidly explore tone and atmosphere in the earlier phases of production. However, our intention has always been for any such assets to be replaced, following final work and review by our art and development teams, with work that aligned with our quality standards and creative direction.

Following reports from our community, we have identified that some of these assets were unintentionally included in the final release. This is not in line with our internal standards, and we take full responsibility for it.

We also acknowledge that we should have clearly disclosed our use of AI. While these tools were primarily used during early production, with the expectation that these assets would be replaced prior to release, we recognize that this does not excuse the lack of transparency.

We sincerely apologize for these oversights.

We are currently conducting a comprehensive audit of all in-game assets and are taking steps to replace any affected content. Updated assets will be rolled out in upcoming patches. In parallel, we are reviewing and strengthening our internal processes to ensure greater transparency and consistency in how we communicate with players moving forward.

value.text:
It seems too many do not understand the list vote at Holyrood - or refuse to do so because of party allegiance.

The SNP list vote will be divided far more than any other party, let alone just the next best placed pro-indy party.

This is why the SNP are polling for ZERO list seats.

See alt text.

images.alt:
Why the SNP list vote is wasted & why you should vote SGP on the list
A five column spreadsheet showing SNP & SGP seats projected by region for Holyrood 2026, per Ballot Box Scotland's aggregated polling tracker.
It shows the SNP list vote will be divided by at least 6, at most 10, whereas in only one region will the SGP list vote be divided at all (Edinburgh & Lothians East), where the one projected FPTP seat means the vote there is divided by two. In contrast, the SNP vote there is divided by six. It demonstrates why SNP polling on the list vote is for zero seats. Their FPTP wins will divide their vote too much to win any.
You get more SGP list MSPs even if the SNP were polling for any, because the SGP list vote is still divided by less, so it takes less votes to get SGP MSPs.
